items.title: Classification and probabilities: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? The class with the highest model score is a probability-certain answer, and accuracy is sufficient even for a rare positive class.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Separate labels, scores, and calibrated probabilities; choose and monitor thresholds using prevalence, error costs, capacity, and slice-specific confusion evidence.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Separate labels, scores, and calibrated probabilities; choose and monitor thresholds using prevalence, error costs, capacity, and slice-specific confusion evidence.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

items.title: Classification and probabilities: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read n binary true labels then n predicted labels. Print tp, fp, tn, fn, precision, and recall to three decimals.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

tests.expectedStdout: tp=1 fp=1 tn=1 fn=2 precision=0.500 recall=0.333
tp=0 fp=0 tn=3 fn=0 precision=0.000 recall=0.000

answer.referenceSolution:
import sys

def solve(data: str) -> str:
    values = list(map(int, data.split())); n = values[0]
    truth = values[1:1+n]; predicted = values[1+n:1+2*n]
    tp = sum(a == 1 and b == 1 for a,b in zip(truth,predicted))
    fp = sum(a == 0 and b == 1 for a,b in zip(truth,predicted))
    tn = sum(a == 0 and b == 0 for a,b in zip(truth,predicted))
    fn = sum(a == 1 and b == 0 for a,b in zip(truth,predicted))
    precision = tp / (tp + fp) if tp + fp else 0.0
    recall = tp / (tp + fn) if tp + fn else 0.0
    return f"tp={tp} fp={fp} tn={tn} fn={fn} precision={precision:.3f} recall={recall:.3f}"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solve(sys.stdin.read()), end="")
answer.explanation: The explicit confusion counts make threshold trade-offs visible and avoid substituting accuracy for rare-class evidence.
